goal: Configure /World/amr/front_cam to match RealSense D435 depth stream for sim-to-real obstacle-layer parity.
thoughts: D435 depth datasheet: 848x480 @ 30 Hz, HFOV ~87°, VFOV ~58°, stereo IR baseline 50 mm. Isaac Sim models clean depth — it does NOT replicate the D435's stereo-IR block-matching noise pattern; be explicit about this gap. Color stream is a SEPARATE camera prim (different optical frame, 1280x720 default). REP-103 optical frame: Z forward, X right, Y down.
code:
spec = lookup_product_spec(product="Intel RealSense D435")
# Use: depth 848x480 @ 30 Hz, HFOV 87°, VFOV 58°, baseline 50 mm, REP-103 optical.

configure_camera(
    prim_path="/World/amr/front_cam",
    resolution=[848, 480],
    hfov_deg=87.0,
    vfov_deg=58.0,
    fps=30,
    output_type="depth",
    frame_id="camera_depth_optical_frame",
    optical_convention="ros_rep103"               # Z fwd, X right, Y down
)

inspect_camera(prim_path="/World/amr/front_cam")

# Color stream is a DIFFERENT prim — do not overload this one:
# configure_camera(prim_path="/World/amr/front_cam_color", resolution=[1280,720], ...)

# Honest gap: Isaac Sim depth is clean. No stereo-IR block-match noise, no post-3m rolloff.
# For realism add omni.replicator depth-noise pass OR accept the sim-to-real delta.
failure_modes: Conflating depth optical frame with color optical frame — TF tree wrong
Claiming Isaac Sim replicates D435 stereo-IR noise pattern (it does not)
Putting color + depth on same prim — only one output_type wins
Intrinsics hand-computed from HFOV alone — drops the VFOV / aspect check
